msgs.text:
I read the prompt as fixing the four-generator tame format: sigma,tau,x,y, with tau^sigma = tau^2 implicit, later generators in the pro-2 core, and profinite exponents represented modulo M=85667662080.The only candidate still alive in the prior log is the dynamic tame-inertia relation variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1i.e.r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,π=π2,η=2π,with π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The prior turns have repeatedly moved the frontier from raw finite tests to a normalized first-IA arithmetic-minus-candidate obstruction. In particular, the latest state is that the residual mixed obstruction for even f=2n is modeled byMd=F2[Cd]≃F2 [T]/(Td−1),d=gcd(e,2n+1),with two additional universal square coordinates, and with gauge actions from normal-basis changes and from replacing τ by τu.New contribution: canonical exact-order projectors for every anti-cyclotomic residual

Turn 62 gave special projectors for d=9:E1=j=0∑8Tj,E3=T+T2+T4+T5+T7+T8,E9=T3+T6.I found the general form. Let d be odd and setAd=F2 [T]/(Td−1).

For every divisor c∣d, defineQc(T)=j=0∑d/c−1Tcj.

Then Qc is the idempotent projecting onto the sum of all character components whose order divides c. Indeed, if ζd=1, thenQc (ζ)={1,0,ord(ζ)∣c,ord(ζ)∤c,because d/c is odd in characteristic 2.

Therefore the canonical exact-order projector isEc(d)(T)=r∣rad(c)∑Qc/r(T)where the sum is over squarefree r dividing c. This is simply inclusion-exclusion, but all signs are + in characteristic 2. Evaluated at a d-th root ζ, Ec(d)(ζ)=1 exactly when ord(ζ)=c, and 0 otherwise.

This recovers Turn 62’s d=9 projectors:Q1=1+T+⋯+T8,Q3=1+T3+T6,Q9=1,soE1=Q1,E3=Q3+Q1,E9=Q9+Q3=T3+T6.Why this helps

The residual R(T)∈F2[Cd] is affected by two gauges:

R(T)↦U(T)R(T),U(T)∈F2[Cd]×,from changing the normal-basis generator, and

R(T)↦R(Tu−1),u∈(Z/d)×,from replacing τ by τu. Turn 62 noted the second gauge explicitly.

The exact-order projectionsEc(d)R are canonical under both gauges in the only way needed for the zero test:

R=0⟺Ec(d)R=0 for every c∣d.Multiplication by a unit cannot turn a zero exact-order component into a nonzero one or vice versa, and T↦Tu preserves exact order. Thus the arithmetic-minus-candidate test can be stated without naming Wedderburn factors:ϵb=ϵS=0andEc(d)R=0 for every c∣d.

For d=9, this is exactly the Turn 62 test with E1,E3,E9. For d=17, it avoids a possible false distinction between the two primitive degree-8 factors: the canonical primitive test is E17(17)R=0, not “which primitive factor is hit.” For d=65, it gives canonical exact-order tests at c=1,5,13,65, even though the primitive 65-part splits into several named irreducible factors after a τ-choice.This does not solve the arithmetic comparison, but it makes the next tests cleaner. Instead of tracking basis-dependent Wedderburn labels, the arithmetic computation should output(ϵb,ϵS;R(T))∈F22⊕F2[Cd],and then apply the canonical exact-order projectors Ec(d). A nonzero exact-order component is a genuine obstruction; vanishing of all exact-order components is exactly the normalized first-IA zero target.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I added a general canonical projector package for the anti-cyclotomic first-IA residual: for Ad=F2[Cd],Qc=j=0∑d/c−1Tcj,Ec(d)=r∣rad(c)∑Qc/r projects onto the exact-order-c residual component. This recovers the d=9 projectors from Turn 62 and gives gauge-stable zero tests for all d.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The arithmetic-minus-candidate residual has not been computed. A dyadic formation theorem or explicit arithmetic comparison is still needed to show that the actual local Galois action has the same normalized filtered affine origins as the candidate over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: For L=Q2(ζ9,ϖ), ϖ9=2, fix τ,σ, and a normal-basis generator. Compute (ϵb,ϵS;R(T)), then test E1(9)

R, E3(9)

R, and E9(9)R. The target remains zero in every component; the projector formalism then scales to later d, such as 17, 13, and 65.[BREAKTHROUGH]
